_metadata.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

upcoming: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 13 (expected Q4 2026)

obligations.source_quote: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way as to ensure that their operation is sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret a system's output and use it appropriately.

decomposed_atoms.description: System operation must be transparent enough for deployers
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Operation SHALL be sufficiently transparent to enable deployers to interpret outputs

decomposed_atoms.description: Deployers must be able to interpret system output
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Transparency SHALL enable deployers to interpret the system's output and use it appropriately

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for interpretability tooling (SHAP, LIME, Captum, ELI5), explanation APIs, feature importance outputs, attention visualizations, and deployer-facing documentation
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the level of transparency is 'sufficient' for the specific use case
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect explainability tooling but cannot assess if transparency is sufficient

obligations.source_quote: An appropriate type and degree of transparency shall be ensured with a view to achieving compliance with the relevant obligations of the provider and deployer set out in Section 3.
obligations.note: Distinct from OBL-1: OBL-1 covers the design requirement (system must be designed for transparency). This obligation covers the outcome requirement — appropriate type and degree of transparency must actually be ensured, measured against Section 3 compliance.

decomposed_atoms.description: The type of transparency must be appropriate
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Transparency SHALL be of an appropriate type for the system and context

decomposed_atoms.description: The degree of transparency must be appropriate
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The degree of transparency SHALL be sufficient to enable compliance with Section 3 obligations
Transparency SHALL be ensured with a view to achieving compliance with provider and deployer obligations in Section 3

automation_assessment.detection_method: Assess whether the level of transparency provided enables compliance with Art. 9-15 and deployer obligations in Section 3
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether transparency type and degree are appropriate for the specific system and context, measured against Section 3 obligations
automation_assessment.rationale: Outcome-based requirement — appropriateness assessment requires legal and domain expertise

obligations.source_quote: High-risk AI systems shall be accompanied by instructions for use in an appropriate digital format or otherwise that include concise, complete, correct and clear information that is relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers.

decomposed_atoms.requirement: System SHALL be accompanied by instructions for use
Instructions SHALL be in an appropriate digital format

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must be concise, complete, correct and clear
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Instructions SHALL include concise, complete, correct and clear information

decomposed_atoms.description: Information must be relevant, accessible and comprehensible
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Information SHALL be relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for existence of deployer-facing documentation (user guide, deployer guide, instructions of use), digital format availability (MD, PDF, HTML), and completeness against Art. 13(3) checklist
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether instructions are truly concise, complete, correct, clear, relevant, accessible and comprehensible
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect documentation existence but cannot assess quality attributes

obligations.source_quote: The instructions for use shall contain at least the following information:

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.13(3)(a): provider identity and contact details
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain identity and contact details of the provider
SHALL contain the intended purpose of the system
SHALL contain the level of accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity metrics
SHALL contain known or foreseeable circumstances that may lead to risks
SHALL contain technical capabilities to explain output (where applicable)
SHALL contain performance regarding specific persons or groups (when appropriate)
SHALL contain specifications for input data (when appropriate)
SHALL contain information to enable deployers to interpret output (where applicable)
SHALL contain changes pre-determined at initial conformity assessment

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.13(3)(d): human oversight measures per Art. 14
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain human oversight measures including technical measures for output interpretation
SHALL contain computational/hardware resources needed, expected lifetime, maintenance measures

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.13(3)(f): log collection mechanisms per Art. 12
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL contain description of mechanisms for deployers to collect/store/interpret logs

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check deployer documentation for provider name, trade name, contact address, or contact information sections
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether all 12 required elements (a-f with sub-items) are adequately covered
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect some documentation sections but cannot verify completeness of all 12 elements
